repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
If a buffer's filetype changes after the LSP client has already
attached (e.g. from json to jsonc via a modeline), but the client
supports both filetypes, it stays attached. It does not notify the
server of the new languageId, causing the server to incorrectly process
the file using the old languageId.

Solution:
Save the languageId used during textDocument/didOpen, and send
textDocument/didClose + textDocument/didOpen when buffer's languageId
changed.

Lsp spec:
https://github.com/microsoft/language-server-protocol/blob/0003fb53f18dc7a4ac7e51d0eb518deeea90fde5/_specifications/lsp/3.18/textDocument/didOpen.md#L5

AI-assisted: Gemini 3.1 Pro

Fix https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/39498.

comment_data.body: When the values were `true` this field was quite self-explanatory, but now I think it merits a description.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -167,7 +167,7 @@ local all_clients = {}
 
 --- @class vim.lsp.Client
 ---
---- @field attached_buffers table<integer,true>
+--- @field attached_buffers table<integer,string>

comment_data.body: Nit: Since we're now handling both branches of the if-statement, I find `if can_start(...)` as the condition easier to read.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -531,9 +531,20 @@ local function lsp_enable_callback(bufnr)
       lsp.is_enabled(client.name)
       -- Check that the client is managed by vim.lsp.config before deciding to detach it!
       and lsp.config[client.name]
-      and not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false)
     then
-      lsp.buf_detach_client(bufnr, client.id)
+      if not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false) then

comment_data.body: With the type change of this PR `old_language_id` will always be a string won't it?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -531,9 +531,20 @@ local function lsp_enable_callback(bufnr)
       lsp.is_enabled(client.name)
       -- Check that the client is managed by vim.lsp.config before deciding to detach it!
       and lsp.config[client.name]
-      and not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false)
     then
-      lsp.buf_detach_client(bufnr, client.id)
+      if not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false) then
+        lsp.buf_detach_client(bufnr, client.id)
+      else
+        -- When switch between lsp supported filetype (e.g. json to jsonc like #39498),
+        -- client should send `textDocument/didClose` + `textDocument/didOpen` with new language id
+        local new_language_id = client.get_language_id(bufnr, vim.bo[bufnr].filetype)
+        local old_language_id = client.attached_buffers[bufnr]
+        if type(old_language_id) == 'string' and old_language_id ~= new_language_id then

comment_data.body:
> will always be a string

Yeah, `nil` is also possible here when you try to attach on the first filetype. 
I remove `type()` wrap.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -531,9 +531,20 @@ local function lsp_enable_callback(bufnr)
       lsp.is_enabled(client.name)
       -- Check that the client is managed by vim.lsp.config before deciding to detach it!
       and lsp.config[client.name]
-      and not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false)
     then
-      lsp.buf_detach_client(bufnr, client.id)
+      if not can_start(bufnr, lsp.config[client.name], false) then
+        lsp.buf_detach_client(bufnr, client.id)
+      else
+        -- When switch between lsp supported filetype (e.g. json to jsonc like #39498),
+        -- client should send `textDocument/didClose` + `textDocument/didOpen` with new language id
+        local new_language_id = client.get_language_id(bufnr, vim.bo[bufnr].filetype)
+        local old_language_id = client.attached_buffers[bufnr]
+        if type(old_language_id) == 'string' and old_language_id ~= new_language_id then

comment_data.body: Looks like in the worst case, languageid might be empty string, not `nil` 👍
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1002,7 +1013,7 @@ function lsp.buf_attach_client(bufnr, client_id)
     return true
   end
 
-  client.attached_buffers[bufnr] = true
+  client.attached_buffers[bufnr] = client.get_language_id(bufnr, vim.bo[bufnr].filetype)

comment_data.body:
need to mention this in news breaking-changes.

not too worried about blast radius, since string is still truthy and `attached_buffers` is not commonly used.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -167,7 +167,8 @@ local all_clients = {}
 
 --- @class vim.lsp.Client
 ---
---- @field attached_buffers table<integer,true>
+--- Each buffer's last used `languageId`.
+--- @field attached_buffers table<integer,string>

comment_data.body:
lint wants this to be `buf`. idk, I might revert that lint rule...

```suggestion
--- @param buf integer Number of the buffer, or 0 for current
function Client:_text_document_did_close_handler(buf)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1124,6 +1125,18 @@ function Client:exec_cmd(cmd, context, handler)
   self:request('workspace/executeCommand', params, handler, context.bufnr)
 end
 
+--- Default handler for the 'textDocument/didClose' LSP notification.
+---
+--- @param bufnr integer Number of the buffer, or 0 for current
+function Client:_text_document_did_close_handler(bufnr)

comment_regular_data.body: This fix reuse `client.attached_buffers[buf]` to store each buffer's last requested languageId, otherwise each `client`x`buffer` will require a new field like `client._last_language_id[buf]`.

would help to add concise comments in the code explaining what is going on.

also pretty sure we have `didClose and didOpen` tests already, can we add asserts into the existing one to avoid 50 lines of new code

> also pretty sure we have `didClose and didOpen` tests already, can we add asserts into the existing one to avoid 50 lines of new code

I fail to find a better place for it, there's indeed didClose/didOpen existed but they are testing different thing (gemini suggested https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/14822b31cfb5a61f175a3ababdfc069af9a8ab71/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua#L818, but it won't reduce code, just reuse a child instance)

I think this extra test is needed, since it describe an lsp behavior in spec.

Backport failed for `release-0.12`, because it was unable to cherry-pick the commit(s).

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin release-0.12
git worktree add -d .worktree/backport-39499-to-release-0.12 origin/release-0.12
cd .worktree/backport-39499-to-release-0.12
git switch --create backport-39499-to-release-0.12
git cherry-pick -x 1e7edb2c52e0485542a300a330b6a66a76fa1ddb
```

title: fix(lsp): send didClose and didOpen when languageId changes
